description: This bar chart compares the size distribution and number density of non-metallic inclusions in low-nitrogen (LN) and high-nitrogen (HN) Fe–28Mn–10Al–0.8C low-density steels. The y-axis reports inclusion number density (# mm⁻²) across size classes from <1 μm to 7–8 μm. The HN steel exhibits dramatically higher inclusion densities in the sub-micrometer and 1–2 μm ranges, peaking near 72 # mm⁻² at 1–2 μm, compared to LN steel which shows a broader distribution with lower peak values around 20 # mm⁻² in the 1–2 and 2–3 μm ranges. Conversely, LN steel contains relatively more inclusions in the 2–4 μm and 7–8 μm categories. These statistical results indicate that nitrogen addition substantially increases the total number density of inclusions while shifting the size distribution toward finer particles. As reported in the associated text, the dominant inclusion in HN steel is AlN, 84% of which is smaller than 2 μm, and these fine inclusions promote austenite grain refinement by acting as heterogeneous nucleation sites.
